FINSTAD Financial Database

2026-06-29

FINSTAD Financial Database

Group 1

  • Aragon, Enrique Miguel Moreno
  • Cardiño, Joaquin Carlo Esteban
  • Carlos, Jay Michael Tenorio
  • Chua, Erin Gabrielle Tan

This is an integrated database containing various explorations and visualizations of data from a Philippine asset, a U.S. asset, and cryptocurrency, against FRED macroeconomic data and the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust. The data analyzed is restricted to January 1, 2020 until December 31, 2025.

Setup

Initialize packages.

pacman::p_load(dplyr, ggplot2, quantmod, tidyverse, tidyquant, lubridate, timetk, zoo)

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(quantmod)
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyquant)
library(lubridate)
library(timetk)
library(zoo)

Comparing the different datasets

We first get symbols from the following sources: - FRED - SPY - Cryptocurrency - U.S. asset - Philippine asset

# date constants for use throughout the project.
DATE_FROM <- "2020-01-01"
DATE_TO <- "2025-12-31"

# macroeconomic data is sourced from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis' FRED dataset.
quantmod::getSymbols("DGS10",
                     src = "FRED",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## [1] "DGS10"
quantmod::getSymbols("CPIAUCSL",
                     src = "FRED",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## [1] "CPIAUCSL"
quantmod::getSymbols("FEDFUNDS",
                     src = "FRED",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## [1] "FEDFUNDS"
# the following symbol data is sourced from yahoo finance.
quantmod::getSymbols("SPY",                 # SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
                     src = "yahoo",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## [1] "SPY"
quantmod::getSymbols("AMD",                 # U.S. Asset: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.
                     src = "yahoo",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## [1] "AMD"
quantmod::getSymbols("PHI",                 # Philippine Asset: PLDT, Inc.
                     src = "yahoo",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## [1] "PHI"
quantmod::getSymbols("^VIX",                # CBOE Volatility Index
                     src = "yahoo",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## Warning: ^VIX contains missing values. Some functions will not work if objects
## contain missing values in the middle of the series. Consider using na.omit(),
## na.approx(), na.fill(), etc to remove or replace them.
## [1] "VIX"
quantmod::getSymbols("ETH-USD",             # Cryptocurrency: Ethereum
                     src = "yahoo",
                     from = DATE_FROM,
                     to = DATE_TO)
## [1] "ETH-USD"

Then we can look at the macroeconomic data,

The data is taken from the Federal Reserve Economic Database (FRED) from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis, where we load in the Market Yield on U.S. Treasury Securities at 10-Year Constant Maturity (DGS10), Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPIAUCSL), and the Federal Funds Effective Rate (FEDFUNDS).

DGS10 is used as a proxy for the “risk-free rate” in finance, it helps investors gauge inflation expectations, economic growth, and monetary policy shifts. CPIAUCSL is seasonally adjusted to gauge monthly inflation metrics on consumer goods. FEDFUNDS is the monthly average interest rate at which banks lend reserves to each other overnight, and reflects the stance of U.S. monetary policy (tightening vs. easing cycles).

These pieces of macroeconomic data would help in understanding broader market cycles and how systemic risks affect stock valuations.

head(DGS10)
##            DGS10
## 2020-01-01    NA
## 2020-01-02  1.88
## 2020-01-03  1.80
## 2020-01-06  1.81
## 2020-01-07  1.83
## 2020-01-08  1.87
head(CPIAUCSL)
##            CPIAUCSL
## 2020-01-01  259.127
## 2020-02-01  259.250
## 2020-03-01  258.076
## 2020-04-01  256.032
## 2020-05-01  255.802
## 2020-06-01  257.042
head(FEDFUNDS)
##            FEDFUNDS
## 2020-01-01     1.55
## 2020-02-01     1.58
## 2020-03-01     0.65
## 2020-04-01     0.05
## 2020-05-01     0.05
## 2020-06-01     0.08

We can then take a look at our chosen financial market data,

U.S. Asset - Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. (AMD) Philippine Asset - PLDT, Inc.(PHI) Cryptocurrency - Etherium (ETH or ETH-USD) Required - State Street SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY)

ETH <- `ETH-USD`

head(AMD)
##            AMD.Open AMD.High AMD.Low AMD.Close AMD.Volume AMD.Adjusted
## 2020-01-02    46.86    49.25   46.63     49.10   80331100        49.10
## 2020-01-03    48.03    49.39   47.54     48.60   73127400        48.60
## 2020-01-06    48.02    48.86   47.86     48.39   47934900        48.39
## 2020-01-07    49.35    49.39   48.04     48.25   58061400        48.25
## 2020-01-08    47.85    48.30   47.14     47.83   53767000        47.83
## 2020-01-09    48.94    49.96   48.39     48.97   76512800        48.97
head(PHI)

## 2020-01-02    19.75    19.80   19.59     19.73      67000     12.94961
## 2020-01-03    19.66    19.82   19.50     19.81      37200     13.00212
## 2020-01-06    19.76    19.95   19.63     19.70      68300     12.92992
## 2020-01-07    19.99    20.54   19.99     20.35     139200     13.35655
## 2020-01-08    20.16    20.59   20.16     20.51     119300     13.46156
## 2020-01-09    20.50    20.99   20.50     20.96     114700     13.75691
head(ETH)
##            ETH-USD.Open ETH-USD.High ETH-USD.Low ETH-USD.Close ETH-USD.Volume
## 2020-01-01     129.6307     132.8354    129.1983      130.8020     7935230330
## 2020-01-02     130.8200     130.8200    126.9549      127.4102     8032709256
## 2020-01-03     127.4113     134.5540    126.4900      134.1717    10476845358
## 2020-01-04     134.1685     136.0527    133.0406      135.0694     7430904515
## 2020-01-05     135.0721     139.4102    135.0456      136.2768     7526675353
## 2020-01-06     136.3055     144.3282    136.0796      144.3042     9093747121
##            ETH-USD.Adjusted
## 2020-01-01         130.8020
## 2020-01-02         127.4102
## 2020-01-03         134.1717
## 2020-01-04         135.0694
## 2020-01-05         136.2768
## 2020-01-06         144.3042
head(SPY)
##            SPY.Open SPY.High SPY.Low SPY.Close SPY.Volume SPY.Adjusted
## 2020-01-02   323.54   324.89  322.53    324.87   59151200     296.1253
## 2020-01-03   321.16   323.64  321.10    322.41   77709700     293.8830
## 2020-01-06   320.49   323.73  320.36    323.64   55653900     295.0042
## 2020-01-07   323.02   323.54  322.24    322.73   40496400     294.1746
## 2020-01-08   322.94   325.78  322.67    324.45   68296000     295.7425
## 2020-01-09   326.16   326.73  325.52    326.65   48473300     297.7478

Lastly, we take a look at the Cboe Volatility Index (VIX), this is not an individual stock but a real time market index created by the Chicago Board Options Exchange (Cboe). This measures the market’s expectation of 30-day volatility in the S&P 500 index.

This adds aid on top of macroeconomic data to further gauge systemic risks, VIX is known as the “Fear Gauge” which reflects levels of anxiety, complacency, or uncertainty in the market, when this “Fear Gauge” level rises it implies larger expected swings in stock prices.

head(VIX)
##            VIX.Open VIX.High VIX.Low VIX.Close VIX.Volume VIX.Adjusted
## 2020-01-02    13.46    13.72   12.42     12.47          0        12.47
## 2020-01-03    15.01    16.20   13.13     14.02          0        14.02
## 2020-01-06    15.45    16.39   13.54     13.85          0        13.85
## 2020-01-07    13.84    14.46   13.39     13.79          0        13.79
## 2020-01-08    15.16    15.24   12.83     13.45          0        13.45
## 2020-01-09    12.95    13.24   12.53     12.54          0        12.54

Cleaning

1. Converting XTS to tibbles

First we convert all XTS objects to tibbles for easier data manipulation.

dgs10_tbl <- tibble(
  date = index(DGS10),
  dgs10 = as.numeric(DGS10$DGS10),
)
cpiaucsl_tbl <- tibble(
  date = index(CPIAUCSL),
  cpiaucsl = as.numeric(CPIAUCSL$CPIAUCSL),
)
spy_tbl <- tibble(
  date = index(SPY),
  open = as.numeric(SPY$SPY.Open),
  high = as.numeric(SPY$SPY.High),
  low = as.numeric(SPY$SPY.Low),
  close = as.numeric(SPY$SPY.Close),
  volume = as.numeric(SPY$SPY.Volume),
  adjusted = as.numeric(SPY$SPY.Adjusted),
  adj_close = as.numeric(Ad(SPY)),
)
amd_tbl <- tibble(
  date = index(AMD),
  open = as.numeric(AMD$AMD.Open),
  high = as.numeric(AMD$AMD.High),
  low = as.numeric(AMD$AMD.Low),
  close = as.numeric(AMD$AMD.Close),
  volume = as.numeric(AMD$AMD.Volume),
  adjusted = as.numeric(AMD$AMD.Adjusted),
  adj_close = as.numeric(Ad(AMD)),
)
phi_tbl <- tibble(
  date = index(PHI),
  open = as.numeric(PHI$PHI.Open),
  high = as.numeric(PHI$PHI.High),
  low = as.numeric(PHI$PHI.Low),
  close = as.numeric(PHI$PHI.Close),
  volume = as.numeric(PHI$PHI.Volume),
  adjusted = as.numeric(PHI$PHI.Adjusted),
  adj_close = as.numeric(Ad(PHI)),
)
vix_tbl <- tibble(
  date = index(VIX),
  open = as.numeric(VIX$VIX.Open),
  high = as.numeric(VIX$VIX.High),
  low = as.numeric(VIX$VIX.Low),
  close = as.numeric(VIX$VIX.Close),
  volume = as.numeric(VIX$VIX.Volume),
  adjusted = as.numeric(VIX$VIX.Adjusted),
  adj_close = as.numeric(Ad(VIX)),
)
eth_tbl <- tibble(
  date = index(ETH),
  open = as.numeric(ETH$`ETH-USD.Open`),
  high = as.numeric(ETH$`ETH-USD.High`),
  low = as.numeric(ETH$`ETH-USD.Low`),
  close = as.numeric(ETH$`ETH-USD.Close`),
  volume = as.numeric(ETH$`ETH-USD.Volume`),
  adjusted = as.numeric(ETH$`ETH-USD.Adjusted`),
  adj_close = as.numeric(Ad(ETH)),
)

2. Asset Identifier

We add an asset identifier per asset to see the data better in table structures.

amd_tbl <- amd_tbl |> 
  mutate(asset = "AMD")

eth_tbl <- eth_tbl |> 
  mutate(asset = "ETH")

phi_tbl <- phi_tbl |> 
  mutate(asset = "PHI")

spy_tbl <- spy_tbl |> 
  mutate(asset = "SPY")

vix_tbl <- vix_tbl |> 
  mutate(asset = "VIX")

3. Computing daily returns

Daily returns are computed using the basic return formula

amd_tbl <- amd_tbl |> 
  mutate(
    return = adj_close / lag(adj_close) - 1
  )

eth_tbl <- eth_tbl |> 
  mutate(
    return = adj_close / lag(adj_close) - 1
  )

phi_tbl <- phi_tbl |> 
  mutate(
    return = adj_close / lag(adj_close) - 1
  )

spy_tbl <- spy_tbl |> 
  mutate(
    return = adj_close / lag(adj_close) - 1
  )

vix_tbl <- vix_tbl |> 
  mutate(
    return = adj_close / lag(adj_close) - 1
  )

4. Removing missing rows

Null rows are dropped to preserve data integrity

amd_tbl <- amd_tbl  |> 
  filter(!is.na(return))

eth_tbl <- eth_tbl  |> 
  filter(!is.na(return))

phi_tbl <- phi_tbl |> 
  filter(!is.na(return))

spy_tbl <- spy_tbl  |> 
  filter(!is.na(return))

vix_tbl <- vix_tbl  |> 
  filter(!is.na(return))

5. Examine the data

glimpse(amd_tbl)
## Rows: 1,506
## Columns: 10
## $ date      <date> 2020-01-03, 2020-01-06, 2020-01-07, 2020-01-08, 2020-01-09,…
## $ open      <dbl> 48.03, 48.02, 49.35, 47.85, 48.94, 49.26, 48.66, 48.64, 48.2…
## $ high      <dbl> 49.39, 48.86, 49.39, 48.30, 49.96, 49.29, 48.86, 49.04, 49.0…
## $ low       <dbl> 47.54, 47.86, 48.04, 47.14, 48.39, 48.00, 48.24, 47.91, 48.1…
## $ close     <dbl> 48.60, 48.39, 48.25, 47.83, 48.97, 48.17, 48.75, 48.21, 48.5…

## $ asset     <chr> "AMD", "AMD", "AMD", "AMD", "AMD", "AMD", "AMD", "AMD", "AMD…
## $ return    <dbl> -0.010183300, -0.004320969, -0.002893147, -0.008704625, 0.02…

Summary Tables

summary(amd_tbl)

##  N.blank  :   0   Median : 0.0000000  
##  Min.nchar:   3   Mean   : 0.0005148  
##  Max.nchar:   3   3rd Qu.: 0.0101548  
##                   Max.   : 0.1673876
summary(spy_tbl)
##       date                 open            high            low       
##  Min.   :2020-01-03   Min.   :228.2   Min.   :229.7   Min.   :218.3  
##  1st Qu.:2021-07-01   1st Qu.:389.9   1st Qu.:392.4   1st Qu.:387.1  
##  Median :2022-12-28   Median :436.9   Median :439.8   Median :434.5  
##  Mean   :2022-12-30   Mean   :457.1   Mean   :459.8   Mean   :454.2  
##  3rd Qu.:2024-06-30   3rd Qu.:533.5   3rd Qu.:536.8   3rd Qu.:530.5  
##  Max.   :2025-12-30   Max.   :690.6   Max.   :691.7   Max.   :689.3  
##      close           volume             adjusted       adj_close    
##  Min.   :222.9   Min.   : 26048700   Min.   :204.4   Min.   :204.4  
##  1st Qu.:389.8   1st Qu.: 57012875   1st Qu.:367.6   1st Qu.:367.6  
##  Median :437.1   Median : 72227400   Median :414.1   Median :414.1  
##  Mean   :457.2   Mean   : 80146318   Mean   :438.5   Mean   :438.5  
##  3rd Qu.:534.5   3rd Qu.: 91523975   3rd Qu.:520.9   3rd Qu.:520.9  
##  Max.   :690.4   Max.   :392220700   Max.   :686.7   Max.   :686.7  
##        asset          return          
##  Length   :1506   Min.   :-0.1094240  
##  N.unique :   1   1st Qu.:-0.0047779  
##  N.blank  :   0   Median : 0.0009785  
##  Min.nchar:   3   Mean   : 0.0006411  
##  Max.nchar:   3   3rd Qu.: 0.0068560  
##                   Max.   : 0.1050194
summary(vix_tbl)
##       date                 open            high            low       
##  Min.   :2020-01-03   Min.   :11.53   Min.   :12.23   Min.   :10.62  
##  1st Qu.:2021-06-30   1st Qu.:16.18   1st Qu.:16.86   1st Qu.:15.44
